description:
About the job

Ready to make your next big professional move? Join us on our journey to achieve our big dream of building the most loved restaurant brands in the world. 

Restaurant Brands International Inc. is one of the world's largest quick service restaurant companies with nearly $45 billion in annual system-wide sales and over 32,000 restaurants in more than 120 countries and territories.

RBI owns four of the world's most prominent and iconic quick service restaurant brands – TIM HORTONS®, BURGER KING®, POPEYES®, and FIREHOUSE SUBS®. These independently operated brands have been serving their respective guests, franchisees and communities for decades. Through its Restaurant Brands for Good framework, RBI is improving sustainable outcomes related to its food, the planet, and people and communities.

RBI is committed to growing the TIM HORTONS®, BURGER KING®, POPEYES® and FIREHOUSE SUBS® brands by leveraging their respective core values, employee and franchisee relationships, and long track records of community support. Each brand benefits from the global scale and shared best practices that come from ownership by Restaurant Brands International Inc.

About RBI

Restaurant Brands International Inc. (RBI) is a global leader in the quick service restaurant industry, managing iconic brands such as Tim Hortons, Burger King, Popeyes, and Firehouse Subs. With over 30,000 restaurants worldwide, RBI is committed to innovation, growth, and building the most beloved restaurant brands. Our dynamic culture empowers employees to take ownership and drive impactful results.

RBI follows a 5 day, in-office work schedule to support collaboration. Successful candidates will be expected to work onsite 5 days per week.

Job Overview

Restaurant Brands International (RBI) is seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Analyst, Brand Communications to join the Burger King Mexico team.

This role is part of the Brand Communications team and supports the execution of integrated marketing communication initiatives across multiple consumer touchpoints. The position works closely with creative, media, and content agencies, as well as internal cross-functional teams, to help ensure timely and high-quality execution of campaigns aligned with brand objectives and the commercial calendar.

The ideal candidate combines a passion for marketing and brand communications with strong organizational, coordination, and analytical skills. This role requires attention to detail, collaboration, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Key Responsibilities


Help ensure consistency across brand communications by adhering to established brand guidelines, tone of voice, and visual identity standards.
Support communication and content development for new product launches in collaboration with agency partners, vendors, and cross-functional teams according to the marketing calendar.
Support the management and tracking of the Brand Communications budget, ensuring accuracy, compliance with internal processes, and alignment with the commercial calendar.
Coordinate project timelines, follow-ups, approvals, and deliverables across multiple ongoing initiatives.
Support campaign performance tracking and reporting in collaboration with agency partners.


Qualifications

Education


Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communications, Finance, Data Analytics, or a related field.


Experience


1–2 years of experience in communication agencies, FMCG/CPG, QSR or retail industries is a plus.
Experience supporting marketing campaigns, budget tracking, or cross-functional projects.
Experience coordinating with internal teams and external partners is a plus.
Exposure to campaign coordination, reporting, or project management is preferred.


Technical Skills


Proficiency in Microsoft Office required, particularly Excel and PowerPoint.
Strong organizational and analytical skills, with the ability to track performance metrics and support reporting processes.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines simultaneously.


Soft Skills


Strong organizational and time management skills with attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including presentation capabilities.
Ability to review creative materials and provide feedback aligned with brand guidelines and campaign objectives.
Collaborative mindset with strong interpersonal skills.
Sense of urgency, accountability, and ownership of assigned responsibilities.
Proactive, adaptable, and solution-oriented approach to work.
Passion for branding, marketing, and delivering impactful consumer experiences.


Language Skills


This position requires conducting business operations across the Latin America region. To perform the essential functions of this role, fluency in both English and Spanish is required.

description:
About the job

Position

Digital Marketing Specialist – Virtual Experiences

Job Description

Arrow Electronics is a global provider of products, services, and solutions to industrial and commercial users of electronic components and enterprise computing solutions. Arrow Electronics guides innovation forward for over 220,000 leading technology manufacturers and service providers. With 2025 sales of $30.9 billion, Arrow develops technology solutions that improve business and daily life. The company maintains 228 locations worldwide with over 85 countries served.

We are looking for a dedicated and detail‑oriented Digital Marketing Specialist – Virtual Experiences to join our Marketing Center of Excellence. In this role, you will be responsible for the end‑to‑end planning, execution, and optimization of webinars and virtual events delivered to global audiences. This position offers an opportunity to work in a dynamic, international environment where operational excellence, process quality, and measurable business impact are key priorities.

What You’ll Be Doing


Planning and executing live webinars and virtual events end‑to‑end, including event setup, registration, live production, recording, and post‑event follow‑up.
Managing event logistics on virtual event platforms such as Microsoft Teams Live, ON24, GoToWebinar, or similar tools, ensuring a seamless attendee and speaker experience.
Building and coordinating event invitations and registrations, including audience segmentation, email communications, reminders, and real‑time registration monitoring.
Training and supporting internal and external speakers through platform guidance, rehearsals, and live technical moderation during events.
Acting as a virtual events platform administrator, maintaining templates, access, troubleshooting issues, and continuously improving engagement features such as polling, Q&A, or captions.
Managing a global virtual events calendar and collaborating with regional marketing teams across different time zones.
Coordinating with external agencies and vendors when additional production support is required, including budget and delivery oversight.
Tracking event performance and preparing post‑event reports covering attendance, engagement metrics, and lead or pipeline attribution.


What We Are Looking For


Hands‑on experience in planning and delivering webinars or virtual events end‑to‑end
Strong operational and process‑driven mindset, with attention to detail and quality standards.
Experience working with virtual event platforms (e.g. Microsoft Teams Live, ON24, GoToWebinar or similar).
Proven ability to manage and support speakers and stakeholders in a live, high‑pressure environment.
Experience working in an international context with cross‑regional teams and time zones.
Solid understanding of post‑event reporting, engagement metrics, and marketing performance tracking.


Work Arrangement

Hybrid: 3 days in office / 2 days work from home

What's In It For You

At Arrow, we recognize that financial rewards and great benefits are important aspects of an ideal job. That’s why we offer competitive financial compensation, including various compensation plans, and a solid benefits package.


30 days of Christmas bonus
40% vacation premium
12 vacation days plus 2 floating days
Vision and Dental Assistance
Life Insurance
Healthcare Insurance
10% Food/Pantry Vouchers
Restaurant Vouchers
13% Savings Fund
Access to Arrow’s Employee Discount Program
Growth Opportunities, and more!


Annual Hiring Range/Hourly Rate

$31,575.00 - $41,580.00 MXN Monthly

Actual compensation offer to candidate may vary from posted hiring range based upon geographic location, work experience, education, and/or skill level. The pay ratio between base pay and target incentive (if applicable) will be finalized at offer.

MX-JAL-Tlaquepaque, Mexico (HPE Guad office)

skills: Mentorship from experienced marketers and direct access to senior leadership
Hands-on work with enterprise platforms (Twilio, SendGrid) from day one
A culture that rewards curiosity, precision, and ownership
Flexible remote or hybrid work arrangement
A real growth path toward a full Lifecycle Marketing Specialist or Marketing Analyst role
Assist in building, scheduling, QAing, and launching email and SMS campaigns using Twilio and SendGrid under senior supervision
Help set up and QA automated lifecycle flows including onboarding sequences, re-engagement campaigns, retention triggers, and other lifecycle marketing initiatives
Support the management of audience lists, segmentation criteria, suppression files, and workflow documentation

Coordinate campaign timelines, internal deliverables, and marketing calendars to ensure campaigns launch on schedule

Help ensure all campaigns comply with applicable regulations including CASL, CAN-SPAM, and GDPR
Assist with list hygiene tasks including managing bounces, opt-outs, and suppression lists
Maintain documentation of campaign workflows, platform settings, operational processes, and process guides
Support ongoing improvements to marketing processes and operational workflows
Work closely with the Director of Marketing and senior team members to align on campaign priorities and goals
Coordinate with marketing, design, and content teams to ensure campaigns are executed accurately, assets are delivered on time, and messaging remains on brand
Participate in team meetings, contribute ideas, and ask questions
Up to 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, digital marketing, or related roles (internships and relevant coursework count)
Basic familiarity with email marketing concepts: deliverability, segmentation, open rates, click-through rates
Comfortable working with data and digital tools; not afraid of dashboards or spreadsheets
Strong attention to detail: you notice what others miss and take quality seriously
Great written communication skills in English
Eagerness to learn marketing platforms and grow into a technical marketing role
Fluency in English (written and spoken). Spanish is a strong asset
Any hands-on experience with an email platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Klaviyo, SendGrid, or similar)
Exposure to SMS marketing or basic understanding of how SMS campaigns work

Studies or coursework in marketing, communications, business, or related fields
Any experience in digital, tech, or startup environments
description:
About the job

We are one of the fastest-growing private youth sports operators in the GTA and Florida. We delivery high-quality, structured sports programming across multiple locations, with a focus on fun, skills development, and exceptional customer experiences.

This is an entry-level role for someone who has foundational knowledge of email or digital marketing and wants to develop deep expertise in lifecycle campaigns, automation, and data-driven optimization. You will work side by side with the Director of Marketing, building real skills in platforms like Twilio and SendGrid while contributing to campaigns that directly impact customer acquisition and retention.

If you are analytical, curious, detail-oriented, and excited about how data and messaging come together to drive results — we want to meet you.

WHY JOIN US

We are a growing company in the sports industry, and our marketing team is at the core of our expansion. This role is an opportunity to go beyond sending emails — you will learn the strategy, the technology, and the data behind lifecycle marketing at a company that moves fast and values results.


Mentorship from experienced marketers and direct access to senior leadership
Hands-on work with enterprise platforms (Twilio, SendGrid) from day one
A culture that rewards curiosity, precision, and ownership
Flexible remote or hybrid work arrangement
A real growth path toward a full Lifecycle Marketing Specialist or Marketing Analyst role


Key Responsibilities

Campaign Execution & Support


Assist in building, scheduling, QAing, and launching email and SMS campaigns using Twilio and SendGrid under senior supervision
Help set up and QA automated lifecycle flows including onboarding sequences, re-engagement campaigns, retention triggers, and other lifecycle marketing initiatives
Support the management of audience lists, segmentation criteria, suppression files, and workflow documentation
Assist in preparing and formatting email templates in collaboration with the design team
Coordinate campaign timelines, internal deliverables, and marketing calendars to ensure campaigns launch on schedule
Maintain organized libraries of marketing assets, templates, and campaign documentation


Testing & Performance Tracking


Support A/B test setup and help document results for subject lines, CTAs, and content variants
Assist with campaign testing, tracking, and reporting across email and SMS channels
Compile and maintain performance reports tracking key metrics including open rates, click-through rates, conversions, engagement, and unsubscribes
Assist in identifying trends in campaign data and flagging opportunities for optimization and process improvements


Compliance & Operations


Help ensure all campaigns comply with applicable regulations including CASL, CAN-SPAM, and GDPR
Assist with list hygiene tasks including managing bounces, opt-outs, and suppression lists
Maintain documentation of campaign workflows, platform settings, operational processes, and process guides
Support ongoing improvements to marketing processes and operational workflows


Collaboration


Work closely with the Director of Marketing and senior team members to align on campaign priorities and goals
Coordinate with marketing, design, and content teams to ensure campaigns are executed accurately, assets are delivered on time, and messaging remains on brand
Participate in team meetings, contribute ideas, and ask questions


Requirements

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS


Up to 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, digital marketing, or related roles (internships and relevant coursework count)
Basic familiarity with email marketing concepts: deliverability, segmentation, open rates, click-through rates
Comfortable working with data and digital tools; not afraid of dashboards or spreadsheets
Strong attention to detail: you notice what others miss and take quality seriously
Great written communication skills in English
Eagerness to learn marketing platforms and grow into a technical marketing role
Fluency in English (written and spoken). Spanish is a strong asset


Preferred Skills & Experience


Any hands-on experience with an email platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Klaviyo, SendGrid, or similar)
Exposure to SMS marketing or basic understanding of how SMS campaigns work
Familiarity with A/B testing concepts
Basic HTML knowledge or willingness to learn email templating
Studies or coursework in marketing, communications, business, or related fields
Any experience in digital, tech, or startup environments


Benefits

Compensation: $800-1200 USD p/m

title: Email Marketing Account Manager
skills: Own a small portfolio of eCommerce client accounts from onboarding through ongoing campaign execution.
Build, test, and optimize email flows and campaigns in Klaviyo to hit client revenue targets.
Review performance and translate the numbers into business metrics clients can act on.

Client-First: You make clients feel genuinely taken care of.

Quick on Your Feet: When a client asks a hard question, you already have an answer.

3+ years as an Email Account Manager or Account Manager
Hands-on Klaviyo experience (a technical assessment is part of this process)
Experience managing eCommerce clients
Strong soft skills in order to guide your team
Excellent English skills (C2 level)
Experience working inside a marketing or email agency
List segmentation and email deliverability experience
Experience using AI tools for copywriting, analysis, or workflow automation
Email design experience

description:
About the job

We are looking for someone who managed Klaviyo accounts and turned underperforming list emails into real revenue.




You'll own your client accounts from kickoff call to monthly results review.




You'll be the person clients call when something isn't working - and you'll fix it.




If this sounds like you, we'd love to have you on our team!




The Role

Own a small portfolio of eCommerce client accounts from onboarding through ongoing campaign execution.
Build, test, and optimize email flows and campaigns in Klaviyo to hit client revenue targets.
Review performance and translate the numbers into business metrics clients can act on.
Serve as the primary point of contact for client calls and issue resolution.
Coach internal team members on email strategy and account management best practices.




This Is for You If...

Client-First: You make clients feel genuinely taken care of.
Sharp Eye: You catch the error before it goes out, every time.
Quick on Your Feet: When a client asks a hard question, you already have an answer.
Ownership-Minded: Your accounts are your accounts - you see them through to results.




Skills & Experience




Must-Haves:

3+ years as an Email Account Manager or Account Manager
Hands-on Klaviyo experience (a technical assessment is part of this process)
Experience managing eCommerce clients
Strong soft skills in order to guide your team
Excellent English skills (C2 level)




Nice-to-Haves:

Experience working inside a marketing or email agency
List segmentation and email deliverability experience
Experience using AI tools for copywriting, analysis, or workflow automation
Email design experience
Analytics experience




What We Offer

Schedule: Remote, Monday-Friday, hours overlapping with Central Time (CST)
Compensation: $3,000-$3,500 USD/month
Team: Small, close-knit team where your wins are everyone's wins




About Us




We're a young, tight-knit team built around great work and good energy.

No corporate layers.

No ego.




Just a group of people who care about email, care about clients, and actually enjoy working together.




You won't be managing 40 accounts here. You'll own a handful, know them inside out, and see what great email marketing actually looks like.




We'd love for you to join us!




Submit your application and we'll be in touch.




Note: Only resumes in English will be taken into consideration.




In the form, please respond "GROTTO" to the "Why should we consider YOU for this position?"

question.




Important Note: This is a contractor position, not an employee role.

title: Marketing Cloud Developer
skills: Develop and maintain marketing automation solutions using Salesforce Marketing Cloud.
Build and customize Email Templates, Cloud Pages, Journeys, and Automations.
Write and troubleshoot AMPscript and Server-Side JavaScript (SSJS) code.
Design and implement data models using Data Extensions, Lists, and APIs.
Integrate Marketing Cloud with other Salesforce clouds (e.g., Sales Cloud, Service Cloud) and third-party platforms via REST and SOAP APIs.
Develop and optimize Journey Builder workflows, Automation Studio processes, and Triggered Sends.
Collaborate with marketing and CRM teams to define and execute campaign strategies.
Perform QA, testing, and deployment of emails, SMS, and push notifications.
Monitor campaign performance and provide recommendations for improvement.
5–6 years of hands-on experience in Salesforce Marketing Cloud development.
Proficiency in AMPscript, SSJS, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Strong understanding of Marketing Cloud Connect, Data Extensions, and Subscriber Management.
Experience with Journey Builder, Automation Studio, Email Studio, and Cloud Pages.
Experience with REST/SOAP APIs for integration with external systems.
Familiarity with SQL queries for segmentation and data extraction.
Knowledge of marketing best practices, data privacy, and CAN-SPAM compliance.
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Salesforce Marketing Cloud certifications (e.g., Marketing Cloud Developer, Marketing Cloud Email Specialist) are highly preferred.
Experience with Einstein features (e.g., Einstein Send Time Optimization, Engagement Scoring).
Familiarity with Agile methodologies.
Exposure to CI/CD tools and version control systems like Git.
Access to e-Learning platforms.
Access to a virtual nutritionist
Amazing people oriented organizational culture
Working from anywhere
Challenging projects using the latest technologies with clients from the US.

title: Sr Mktg Serv Spec
skills: Developing Insights
Analyze brand execution data, omnichannel metrics, and tactical KPIs to identify trends, risks, and early signals requiring escalation.
Partner with Commercial Excellence / Commercial Operations to develop reports, dashboards, and tactical performance analyses with a strong focus on omnichannel execution.
Identify operational opportunities and gaps to optimize in‑market performance.
Provide analytical support to demand forecasting, scenario development, and market assessment activities led by Product Managers.
Maintain a solid understanding of therapy areas and brand strategies to properly contextualize insights.
Brand & Content Management
Provide operational support to brand message architecture defined by Product Managers for core products and pipeline assets.
Coordinate and control promotional materials and digital content using approved platforms (e.g., Veeva PromoMats).
Act as single point of contact (SPOC) with digital and creative agencies across brands.
Ensure content is harmonized, standardized, and aligned with the approved brand narrative across channels.
Support global‑to‑local material adaptation processes in alignment with local strategy and compliance requirements.
Execution Excellence
Execute and coordinate operational marketing activities with vendors, agencies, and internal partners.
Manage end‑to‑end marketing processes including budgets, POs, RFAs, WOs, and vendor interactions.
Track Business Unit marketing spend, ensuring correct execution versus plan and timely reporting.
Organize meetings, congresses, and product‑sponsored events, coordinating logistics with Events, Medical, and Commercial Operations.
Ensure consistent, efficient, and compliant execution of all marketing activities.
Cross‑Functional & Business Unit Support
Work closely with Product Managers, Medical Affairs, Finance, Procurement, Events, and Commercial Operations to ensure seamless coordination.
Support Business Unit governance through meeting preparation, materials, agendas, and follow‑up actions.
Prepare and distribute internal communications related to brand initiatives, meetings, and events.
Manage projects with increasing autonomy, applying analytical judgment and execution discipline.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving capabilities with ability to translate data into insights.
Excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail and execution rigor.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced, matrixed environment.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
High level of professionalism, resilience, and sound judgment.
Strong proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Outlook.
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Life Sciences, Engineering, or related field.
3–5+ years of experience in marketing analytics, marketing operations, or commercial roles within pharmaceutical or healthcare environments.
Demonstrated experience supporting brand execution, analytics, budgeting, and agency/vendor coordination.
Fluency in English and Spanish (written and spoken).
description:
About the job

At Gilead, we’re creating a healthier world for all people. For more than 35 years, we’ve tackled diseases such as HIV, viral hepatitis, COVID-19 and cancer – working relentlessly to develop therapies that help improve lives and to ensure access to these therapies across the globe. We continue to fight against the world’s biggest health challenges, and our mission requires collaboration, determination and a relentless drive to make a difference.

Every member of Gilead’s team plays a critical role in the discovery and development of life-changing scientific innovations. Our employees are our greatest asset as we work to achieve our bold ambitions, and we’re looking for the next wave of passionate and ambitious people ready to make a direct impact.

We believe every employee deserves a great leader. People Leaders are the cornerstone to the employee experience at Gilead and Kite. As a people leader now or in the future, you are the key driver in evolving our culture and creating an environment where every employee feels included, developed and empowered to fulfil their aspirations. Join Gilead and help create possible, together.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities


 Developing Insights
Analyze brand execution data, omnichannel metrics, and tactical KPIs to identify trends, risks, and early signals requiring escalation.
Partner with Commercial Excellence / Commercial Operations to develop reports, dashboards, and tactical performance analyses with a strong focus on omnichannel execution.
Identify operational opportunities and gaps to optimize in‑market performance.
Provide analytical support to demand forecasting, scenario development, and market assessment activities led by Product Managers.
Maintain a solid understanding of therapy areas and brand strategies to properly contextualize insights.
 Brand & Content Management
Provide operational support to brand message architecture defined by Product Managers for core products and pipeline assets.
Coordinate and control promotional materials and digital content using approved platforms (e.g., Veeva PromoMats).
Act as single point of contact (SPOC) with digital and creative agencies across brands.
Ensure content is harmonized, standardized, and aligned with the approved brand narrative across channels.
Support global‑to‑local material adaptation processes in alignment with local strategy and compliance requirements.
 Execution Excellence
Execute and coordinate operational marketing activities with vendors, agencies, and internal partners.
Manage end‑to‑end marketing processes including budgets, POs, RFAs, WOs, and vendor interactions.
Track Business Unit marketing spend, ensuring correct execution versus plan and timely reporting.
Organize meetings, congresses, and product‑sponsored events, coordinating logistics with Events, Medical, and Commercial Operations.
Ensure consistent, efficient, and compliant execution of all marketing activities.
 Cross‑Functional & Business Unit Support
Work closely with Product Managers, Medical Affairs, Finance, Procurement, Events, and Commercial Operations to ensure seamless coordination.
Support Business Unit governance through meeting preparation, materials, agendas, and follow‑up actions.
Prepare and distribute internal communications related to brand initiatives, meetings, and events.
Manage projects with increasing autonomy, applying analytical judgment and execution discipline.


Competencies & Skills


Strong analytical and problem‑solving capabilities with ability to translate data into insights.
Excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail and execution rigor.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced, matrixed environment.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
High level of professionalism, resilience, and sound judgment.
Strong proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Outlook.


Education & Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Life Sciences, Engineering, or related field.
3–5+ years of experience in marketing analytics, marketing operations, or commercial roles within pharmaceutical or healthcare environments.
Demonstrated experience supporting brand execution, analytics, budgeting, and agency/vendor coordination.
Fluency in English and Spanish (written and spoken).


For Current Gilead Employees And Contractors

description:
About the job

Company Introduction

WorldStrides is the global leader in educational travel and experiential learning. The company was founded in 1967 to provide middle school travel programs to Washington, D.C. and has grown to provide a wide range of programs for more than half a million students annually to over 100 countries around the world. WorldStrides offers experiential learning programs in educational travel, performing arts, language immersion, career exploration, service-learning, study abroad, and sports. Each of these experiences helps students to see beyond the classroom and to see the world – and themselves – in new ways.

Job Description

The Business Development Representative (BDR) / Lead Qualifier is responsible for generating new business opportunities through high-volume outbound calls and email outreach to schools and teachers across the U.S.

In this role, you will: Proactively prospect and engage educators to introduce WorldStrides programs, qualify leads by identifying teachers interested in organizing student travel experiences, consistently meet and exceed monthly targets for calls and qualified leads, and collaborate with the Sales team by passing high-quality opportunities for closing.

This is a goal-driven, outbound sales role where success depends on your ability to handle rejection and stay motivated, build rapport quickly over the phone, maintain strong activity levels daily, and meet weekly and monthly goals in order to achieve additional compensation through a set commission structure.

You’ll work with a wide range of educational travel programs (history, science, national parks, and more), giving you constant opportunities to connect with new prospects and refine your sales and communication skills.

Responsibilities


Make calls and leave messages for teachers nationwide and use talk tracks for live calls.
Send follow-up emails.
Work on special projects, including periodic competitive calls, calls to unassigned schools, specialty schools and other telemarketing efforts.
Periodically research schools to help identify top prospects and maintain the database. Use templates to create and send flyers and update PowerPoint presentations.


Qualifications


Desired work experience in sales, marketing, promotions or telemarketing.
Proficiency in relevant computer applications including CRM.
Desired knowledge of customer service/sales principles and practices.
Six months or more experience in sales/lead creation/lead qualifying with Telephone sales experience preferred
Good data entry/keyboard skills
Familiar with office rules/office environment
High school diploma or GED required
Advanced (85%+) English level
Desired knowledge of sales and marketing principles and strategies
Proactive
Organized


Work Perks


Fun & driven environment.
Excellent medical, dental, and vision coverage, life, accidental death and dismemberment, accident, critical illness, and disability insurance.
+10% of gross salary as food stamps.
1 paid volunteer day & 25 PTO* days to start – accrue up to 28 over 3 years, 4 mental health days, and 5 bereavement days.
Statutory Holidays by Mexican Federal Labor Law.
Tuition reimbursement up to five thousand, two hundred fifty dollars annually or one thousand dollars towards professional certification annually.
Flexible work schedule providing on-site and hybrid opportunities.
Encouraged participation in our Employee Resource Groups and Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion council.
Fitness opportunities. Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Paid statutory parental leave.


WorldStrides, a global organization, is committed to educate and serve communities worldwide. Our commitment is fueled by the passion of our team members and partners to make experiential learning accessible, while also being socially, environmentally, and ethically responsible. Together, we accomplish this by investing in initiatives to promote inclusion, diversity, and sustainability.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, WorldStrides is committed to building a diverse workforce, supported by an environment that promotes inclusion and belonging. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.

WorldStrides will only employ those who are legally authorized to work in the United States. This is not a position for which sponsorship will be provided. Individuals with temporary visas such as E, F-1, J-1, H-1, H-2, L, B, J or TN, or who need sponsorship for work authorization now or in the future, are not eligible for hire. Select seasonal roles may consider students on J-1 or F-1 visas.

description:
About the job

Sobre nosotros

En Koltin, estamos rediseñando la forma en que cuidamos a nuestros papás, mamás, abuelos y abuelas, con el objetivo de ayudarles a mantenerse saludables, activos e independientes durante el mayor tiempo posible. Lo hacemos a través de nuestras membresías de salud, que integran:

Atención clínica enfocada en la prevención
Cobertura médica de gastos mayores
Programas de bienestar y comunidad

Somos la primera empresa en México que protege a personas adultas mayores, desde los 50 hasta los 84 años. Estamos creciendo rápidamente en todo el país, con un propósito claro: transformar la experiencia de salud para las Grandes Personas, haciéndola más humana, accesible y digna.




Sobre el puesto

Forma parte del equipo de Lifecycle, el área que convierte el backlog de leads de Koltin en ventas. Tu trabajo es en conjunto con el líder del área armar la estrategia y llevar a cabo la ejecución: operar los flujos de CRM, contactar leads en el momento correcto y asegurarse de que los que están listos para comprar lleguen al equipo de ventas.

Este no es un rol de soporte. Es un rol de conversión. Cada lead que reactiva y lleva al equipo de ventas representa una venta real — y eso se mide cada semana.




Responsabilidades

Ejecutar los flujos de reactivación del backlog diariamente según el playbook del Lead de CRM
Contactar leads de forma proactiva por los canales definidos (email, WhatsApp, llamada)
Calificar leads: identificar quién está listo para pasar al equipo de guías de ventas
Registrar todas las interacciones en HubSpot con precisión — tus datos alimentan las decisiones del equipo
Detectar qué mensajes y acciones funcionan mejor y reportarlo al Lead de CRM
Proponer mejoras al playbook cuando algo no funciona




Eres un buen fit si eres una persona que tiene

1-2 años en CRM, data analytics
Experiencia con HubSpot u otra herramienta de CRM
Orden y detalle: registras bien, no te saltas pasos
Comunicación escrita clara, empática y adaptada al segmento 60+
Capacidad de manejar volumen sin perder calidad de contacto
Orientación a resultados: sabes si te está yendo bien o no
Autonomía para priorizar sin que te digan qué hacer cada hora
Apertura para aprender e iterar rápido
Deseable: manejo de SQL




Que te ofrecemos

Rol con impacto directo — tus acciones se ven en el número de ventas cada semana
Equipo chico con dinámica de startup: aprendizaje rápido, decisión ágil
Remoto 100%
Sueldo competitivo + variable por meta de conversión
Proyección clara: este rol puede crecer hacia lead de CRM o roles de growth




Beneficios residiendo en México

🎓 Acceso a herramientas y beneficios para tu crecimiento profesional, como cursos, talleres, libros, etc.
💊 Asistencia médica privada por videollamada (médico general, nutriólogo y psicólogo) ilimitado para ti y 3 familiares

title: Email + SMS Project Manager
skills: Own the production timeline for monthly email campaigns across a portfolio of DTC clients, tracking every deliverable from brief through deployment, ensuring nothing ships late or half-baked.
Coordinate across strategists, copywriters, and designers to keep tasks moving through each stage of production, following up before deadlines slip rather than after.
Manage client communication for asset collection and approvals, gathering product imagery, promotional details, brand updates, and signoffs on time so campaigns stay on schedule.
Take detailed, actionable notes during client calls and internal meetings, documenting decisions, action items, and follow-ups so nothing gets lost between conversations.
Monitor flow and campaign progress in Asana, keeping boards clean, tasks current, and stakeholders informed on status without being asked.
Flag risks and blockers early – if a campaign is at risk of missing its send date, the team hears it from you first, along with a proposed path forward.
Support strategists in preparing for client calls by pulling together performance snapshots, campaign calendars, and any relevant context ahead of meetings.
Use AI tools (we use Claude) to streamline your workflow – drafting follow-ups, organizing meeting notes, building status updates, and finding faster ways to manage recurring processes.
2+ years of experience in project management, preferably at a marketing agency, eCommerce brand, or in a role managing multi-stakeholder creative production.
Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with overlapping deadlines without dropping balls.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, you can follow up firmly without burning relationships, and your Slack messages are clear, not novels.
Excellent organizational instincts – you build systems and checklists, not just to-do lists.
Experience with project management tools (Asana strongly preferred) and comfort navigating Slack, Google Workspace, and Figma at a basic level.
High attention to detail – you catch the missing link in the message and the task that was marked done but wasn't.
description:
About the job

About the role

As an Email + SMS Project Manager at Homestead, you are the operational backbone of our lifecycle marketing team. You keep campaigns moving, deadlines tight, and nothing falling through the cracks – across a portfolio of DTC brands running monthly email campaigns, automated flows, and SMS programs.

You'll sit at the intersection of strategy, copy, design, and client communication. Your job is to make sure the right assets are in the right hands at the right time, approvals don't stall, and every campaign ships on time and at standard. When something's behind, you flag it before anyone else notices. When a client is slow on approvals, you follow up with the right mix of urgency and professionalism. When a strategist needs something pulled together for a call, you've already got it.




Responsibilities

Own the production timeline for monthly email campaigns across a portfolio of DTC clients, tracking every deliverable from brief through deployment, ensuring nothing ships late or half-baked.
Coordinate across strategists, copywriters, and designers to keep tasks moving through each stage of production, following up before deadlines slip rather than after.
Manage client communication for asset collection and approvals, gathering product imagery, promotional details, brand updates, and signoffs on time so campaigns stay on schedule.
Take detailed, actionable notes during client calls and internal meetings, documenting decisions, action items, and follow-ups so nothing gets lost between conversations.
Monitor flow and campaign progress in Asana, keeping boards clean, tasks current, and stakeholders informed on status without being asked.
Flag risks and blockers early – if a campaign is at risk of missing its send date, the team hears it from you first, along with a proposed path forward.
Support strategists in preparing for client calls by pulling together performance snapshots, campaign calendars, and any relevant context ahead of meetings.
Use AI tools (we use Claude) to streamline your workflow – drafting follow-ups, organizing meeting notes, building status updates, and finding faster ways to manage recurring processes.




Qualifications

2+ years of experience in project management, preferably at a marketing agency, eCommerce brand, or in a role managing multi-stakeholder creative production.
Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with overlapping deadlines without dropping balls.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, you can follow up firmly without burning relationships, and your Slack messages are clear, not novels.
Excellent organizational instincts – you build systems and checklists, not just to-do lists.
Experience with project management tools (Asana strongly preferred) and comfort navigating Slack, Google Workspace, and Figma at a basic level.
High attention to detail – you catch the missing link in the message and the task that was marked done but wasn't.

title: Email Marketing Specialist
skills: Build, test, and deploy email campaigns using Klaviyo, ensuring accuracy, functionality, and responsiveness across devices and email clients.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks before campaign launches, validating links, dynamic content, segmentation, and personalization.
Execute A/B testing for subject lines, content, audience segmentation, and send-time optimization.
Build and maintain automated lifecycle journeys, including welcome series, onboarding, abandoned cart, post-purchase, cross-sell, loyalty, win-back, and re-engagement campaigns.
Translate CRM strategies into scalable automated workflows using behavioral triggers, conditional logic, and customer segmentation.
Continuously optimize customer journeys to improve engagement and conversion rates.
Manage customer segments, profile properties, suppression lists, and personalization variables within Klaviyo.
Support integrations with marketing and customer data platforms to maintain accurate audience targeting and campaign execution.
Ensure data integrity across CRM systems and marketing platforms.
Monitor and analyze campaign performance, including deliverability, open rates, click-through rates, conversion rates, revenue attribution, and engagement metrics.
Identify trends and provide actionable recommendations to improve campaign effectiveness and customer retention.
Apply email marketing best practices related to deliverability, frequency management, compliance, personalization, and subscriber engagement.
Partner with Marketing, Creative, Analytics, and E-commerce teams to execute campaigns aligned with business objectives.
Support ongoing testing initiatives and contribute to continuous improvements in CRM and lifecycle marketing strategies.
2–4 years of experience in Email Marketing, CRM Operations, or Lifecycle Marketing.
Experience working in DTC, e-commerce, subscription-based, or consumer-focused businesses.
Advanced proficiency with Klaviyo, including campaigns, flows, segmentation, personalization variables, reporting, and automation.
Strong understanding of lifecycle marketing concepts, including customer onboarding, retention, re-engagement, loyalty, cross-sell, and win-back strategies.
Experience creating audience segments and leveraging behavioral data to personalize customer communications.
Familiarity with HTML and CSS for basic email template modifications.
Strong analytical skills with experience interpreting campaign performance data and translating insights into recommendations.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.
Advanced English proficiency (C1), with strong verbal and written communication skills.
Experience with additional CRM or marketing automation platforms such as HubSpot, Braze, Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, or similar.
Experience integrating CRM platforms with customer data solutions or marketing technologies.
Knowledge of deliverability best practices, email compliance, and testing methodologies.
Experience working in fast-paced, cross-functional marketing environments.
Competitive compensation in USD.
Access to learning platforms and professional development opportunities.
Flexible work environment.
Collaborative and multicultural teams.
description:
About the job

At EX Squared LATAM, we are a digital transformation company focused on building innovative, high-impact technology solutions that drive real business value. We partner with top-tier clients across global, regional, and local markets, fostering a collaborative, inclusive, and innovation-driven culture where your ideas truly matter.




Role Overview

EX Squared LATAM is seeking an Email Marketing Specialist to support a fast-growing direct-to-consumer (DTC) e-commerce brand by executing high-impact email marketing campaigns and customer lifecycle programs. This role will be responsible for building, testing, optimizing, and deploying email campaigns and automated customer journeys that drive engagement, retention, and revenue.




The ideal candidate has hands-on experience with Klaviyo, a strong understanding of lifecycle marketing, and an analytical mindset focused on continuous optimization through data-driven decision-making.




Location: Fully remote position within LATAM.




Contract Duration: Fixed term (6 months)




Language Requirement: Advanced English.




What you'll do




Campaign Execution

Build, test, and deploy email campaigns using Klaviyo, ensuring accuracy, functionality, and responsiveness across devices and email clients.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks before campaign launches, validating links, dynamic content, segmentation, and personalization.
Execute A/B testing for subject lines, content, audience segmentation, and send-time optimization.




Lifecycle Marketing

Build and maintain automated lifecycle journeys, including welcome series, onboarding, abandoned cart, post-purchase, cross-sell, loyalty, win-back, and re-engagement campaigns.
Translate CRM strategies into scalable automated workflows using behavioral triggers, conditional logic, and customer segmentation.
Continuously optimize customer journeys to improve engagement and conversion rates.




Audience & Data Management

Manage customer segments, profile properties, suppression lists, and personalization variables within Klaviyo.
Support integrations with marketing and customer data platforms to maintain accurate audience targeting and campaign execution.
Ensure data integrity across CRM systems and marketing platforms.




Performance Analysis & Optimization

Monitor and analyze campaign performance, including deliverability, open rates, click-through rates, conversion rates, revenue attribution, and engagement metrics.
Identify trends and provide actionable recommendations to improve campaign effectiveness and customer retention.
Apply email marketing best practices related to deliverability, frequency management, compliance, personalization, and subscriber engagement.




Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with Marketing, Creative, Analytics, and E-commerce teams to execute campaigns aligned with business objectives.
Support ongoing testing initiatives and contribute to continuous improvements in CRM and lifecycle marketing strategies.




What you'll bring

2–4 years of experience in Email Marketing, CRM Operations, or Lifecycle Marketing.
Experience working in DTC, e-commerce, subscription-based, or consumer-focused businesses.
Advanced proficiency with Klaviyo, including campaigns, flows, segmentation, personalization variables, reporting, and automation.
Strong understanding of lifecycle marketing concepts, including customer onboarding, retention, re-engagement, loyalty, cross-sell, and win-back strategies.
Experience creating audience segments and leveraging behavioral data to personalize customer communications.
Familiarity with HTML and CSS for basic email template modifications.
Strong analytical skills with experience interpreting campaign performance data and translating insights into recommendations.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.
Advanced English proficiency (C1), with strong verbal and written communication skills.




What will make you stand out

Experience with additional CRM or marketing automation platforms such as HubSpot, Braze, Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, or similar.
Experience integrating CRM platforms with customer data solutions or marketing technologies.
Knowledge of deliverability best practices, email compliance, and testing methodologies.
Experience working in fast-paced, cross-functional marketing environments.




Why EX Squared?

At EX Squared, we believe in building strong, collaborative teams that create meaningful impact. You’ll work alongside talented professionals across the region, contributing to innovative projects while continuing to grow your career in a supportive, flexible, and people-focused environment.




We value curiosity, ownership, continuous learning, and open collaboration, creating an environment where professionals can thrive, grow, and make a difference.




What We Offer

Competitive compensation in USD.
Access to learning platforms and professional development opportunities.
Flexible work environment.
Collaborative and multicultural teams.




Eligibility Note

This opportunity is only available to candidates currently residing in Latin America or Brazil. Applications outside these regions will not be considered.




Ready to become an EXpert?

Join our EXperts team! #ApplyNow

title: Marketing Data Analyst
skills: Develop analytical solutions and reporting strategies to support client marketing initiatives and business objectives
Perform quantitative analysis, customer segmentation, predictive modeling, and campaign performance measurement using SQL, SAS, or similar tools
Analyze large datasets from multiple sources to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for campaign optimization
Create client-facing presentations in PowerPoint and translate analytical findings into clear business recommendations
Partner with Program Management and IT teams to support data architecture analysis and improve marketing program effectiveness
2+ years of experience in marketing analytics, business analytics, or data analysis roles
Hands-on experience with SQL and/or SAS for data extraction, analysis, and reporting
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, including development of executive-level presentations
Experience with statistical analysis, regression modeling, segmentation, testing methodologies, and campaign measurement
Experience with Salesforce, Einstein Analytics, Datorama, Snowflake, Oracle, or SQL Server is preferred
Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Statistics, Marketing, Business, Computer Science, or a related field
description:
About the job

Job Title

Marketing Data Analyst

Job Description

We're Concentrix. The intelligent transformation partner. Solution-focused. Tech-powered. Intelligence-fueled.

The global technology and services leader that powers the world’s best brands, today and into the future. We’re solution-focused, tech-powered, intelligence-fueled. With unique data and insights, deep industry expertise, and advanced technology solutions, we’re the intelligent transformation partner that powers a world that works, helping companies become refreshingly simple to work, interact, and transact with. We shape new game-changing careers in over 70 countries, attracting the best talent.

The Concentrix Technical Products and Services team is the driving force behind Concentrix’s transformation, data, and technology services. We integrate world-class digital engineering, creativity, and a deep understanding of human behavior to find and unlock value through tech-powered and intelligence-fueled experiences. We combine human-centered design, powerful data, and strong tech to accelerate transformation at scale. You will be surrounded by the best in the world providing market leading technology and insights to modernize and simplify the customer experience. Within our professional services team, you will deliver strategic consulting, design, advisory services, market research, and contact center analytics that deliver insights to improve outcomes and value for our clients. Hence achieving our vision.

Our game-changers around the world have devoted their careers to ensuring every relationship is exceptional. And we’re proud to be recognized with awards such as "World's Best Workplaces," “Best Companies for Career Growth,” and “Best Company Culture,” year after year.

Join us and be part of this journey towards greater opportunities and brighter futures.

Job Summary

We are seeking a Marketing Data Analyst to support data-driven marketing initiatives by transforming complex datasets into actionable business insights. This role will work closely with clients and internal teams to analyze campaign performance, identify customer behavior trends, and develop presentations that clearly communicate findings and recommendations. The ideal candidate has hands-on experience with SQL or SAS, marketing analytics, and creating client-facing reports and PowerPoint presentations.

Responsibilities


 Develop analytical solutions and reporting strategies to support client marketing initiatives and business objectives
 Perform quantitative analysis, customer segmentation, predictive modeling, and campaign performance measurement using SQL, SAS, or similar tools
 Analyze large datasets from multiple sources to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for campaign optimization
 Create client-facing presentations in PowerPoint and translate analytical findings into clear business recommendations
 Partner with Program Management and IT teams to support data architecture analysis and improve marketing program effectiveness


Qualifications


 2+ years of experience in marketing analytics, business analytics, or data analysis roles
 Hands-on experience with SQL and/or SAS for data extraction, analysis, and reporting
 Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, including development of executive-level presentations
 Experience with statistical analysis, regression modeling, segmentation, testing methodologies, and campaign measurement
 Experience with Salesforce, Einstein Analytics, Datorama, Snowflake, Oracle, or SQL Server is preferred
 Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Statistics, Marketing, Business, Computer Science, or a related field

description:
About the job

Join #TeamViva as our next Loyalty Commercial Lead!




At Viva, we look for passionate individuals who truly love what they do. Now is the perfect time to join our team!

Viva is the lowest-cost, most preferred, fun, and profitable airline in the Americas, operating the youngest fleet in Latin America and ranking among the fastest-growing airlines worldwide. We foster a culture of inclusion, passion, and transparency—breaking traditional norms, redefining the way we work, and committing to excellence in service and passenger safety.




About the role

As a Loyalty Commercial Lead, you will own the commercial strategy, partnership portfolio, and revenue performance of the Financial Institutions vertical, managing cobrand and points transfer partnerships with banks, card networks, and fintech partners.

This role involves overseeing multi-partner relationships, driving portfolio growth and commercial performance, managing complex partnership structures, and leading executive-level engagement with key financial institutions as the main commercial owner of the vertical.




Your deliverables will include:

• Define and execute the commercial strategy for the Financial Institutions vertical, managing revenue performance, partnership growth, and portfolio optimization.

• Manage and strengthen relationships with banking partners, financial institutions, fintechs, and payment platforms, including cobrand and points transfer partnerships.

• Lead commercial negotiations, partnership governance, and executive-level engagement with key stakeholders and C-level counterparts.

• Develop business cases, financial models, and strategic recommendations to support new partnerships, portfolio expansion, and revenue growth initiatives.

• Oversee cross-functional collaboration with Legal, Finance, Product, Tech, and Marketing teams to ensure successful partnership integration and execution.

• Monitor portfolio KPIs, partnership performance, and financial metrics, identifying risks and driving strategic actions to optimize business results.




Our ideal candidate:

• Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Marketing, Engineering, or related field.

• Advanced English proficiency required.

• 3-6 years of experience in business development, loyalty, cobrand partnerships, banking, fintech, or related commercial roles.

• Experience working with banks, fintech companies, startups, or payment platforms.

• Strong commercial and negotiation skills, including sales strategy, partner management, and executive presentations.

• Analytical mindset with the ability to evaluate business performance, market opportunities, and commercial data.

• Experience in ecommerce, loyalty programs, cobrand products, or financial partnerships is highly preferred.

• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and interact with senior leadership.

• Ability to manage multiple conversations, priorities, and negotiations in fast-paced environments.

• Knowledge of Tableau and Microsoft Office tools preferred.




Benefits You Will Enjoy as #TeamViva

Flexible schedule
#TheBestAirlineToWork: An incredible work environment
Unlimited vacation from day one
Savings fund & savings account
Staff Travel: Discounted flights for you and up to 12 loved ones after 6 months
MyIDTravel: Fly worldwide with 20+ airline alliances at reduced fares after 6 months
Life & medical insurance + 24/7 online family medical assistance
Exclusive discounts on gyms, retail stores, restaurants, universities, and more
Above-the-law benefits




Viva is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without discrimination of any kind. All candidate information will be handled confidentially and in compliance with Mexican data-protection regulations. Your data will be used only for recruitment-related purposes.

skills: Quality Assurance: Quality check campaign execution by the advertising team, catching errors in setup, targeting, copy, creative, event dates, locations, and more.

Strategy & Reporting: Create strategic and budget recommendations for campaigns and develop regular reports with actionable insights for clients.
Project Management: Ensure meeting action items are carefully noted, assigned, and tracked in ClickUp to ensure timely execution.

Foundational SEO optimization skills across client web domains.
Join a boutique, fast-paced team where your work directly moves the needle for high-quality clients.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Digital Ad Buyer & Quality Control

Location: Remote (Latin America)

Work Hours: Full-Time

Compensation: $2,500–$3,000 USD/month




About The Role

We are seeking a highly detailed and hands-on Digital Ad Buyer & Quality Control specialist to support our performance marketing team. Our client is a boutique digital marketing agency that prioritizes substance over fluff, maintaining a close, high-impact relationship with a limited roster of premium clients across music, entertainment, media, and tech.

This role is structured in phases: initially, your primary focus will be performing meticulous quality checks and reporting across paid marketing, email, and SMS campaigns. Within a month, you will transition into independently planning, setting up, and launching ad campaigns across multiple platforms. If you are an incredibly organized stickler for detail who loves a fast-paced environment, this role is for you.




Key Responsibilities

Quality Assurance: Quality check campaign execution by the advertising team, catching errors in setup, targeting, copy, creative, event dates, locations, and more.
Communications & Tracking: Monitor and respond to pertinent email communications between clients and external teams.
Campaign Execution: Build, launch, and manage end-to-end paid ad campaigns without assistance across Meta and TikTok (and ideally Google).
Strategy & Reporting: Create strategic and budget recommendations for campaigns and develop regular reports with actionable insights for clients.
Project Management: Ensure meeting action items are carefully noted, assigned, and tracked in ClickUp to ensure timely execution.




Must-Have Qualifications

Experience: 3+ years of experience building and managing ad campaigns, with deep expertise in the nuances of the Meta ads platform at a micro level, alongside experience in TikTok and Google Ads.
Project Management Tools: Proficiency using in-depth project management platforms such as ClickUp, Asana, or similar software.
Extreme Attention to Detail: Absolute zero-tolerance for typos, inaccurate information, or mismatched event details (such as dates, venues, or lineups).
Communication Skills: Proactive internal and external communication skills with fluent English proficiency.
Team Fit: Experience working closely with a small, fast-paced team while remaining highly organized with your own tasks, priorities, and calendar.




Nice-to-Have Qualifications

Experience working with ticketed live events, a passion for music, or strong taste in the space.
Hands-on technical experience with tracking pixels (generation, installation, conversion events, and custom audience creation).
Proficiency with Google Analytics to monitor client traffic trends.
Experience drafting and executing email or SMS marketing campaigns using platforms like Mailchimp, Klaviyo, Laylo, Hive, or Community.
Foundational SEO optimization skills across client web domains.




Why Work With Us?

Join a boutique, fast-paced team where your work directly moves the needle for high-quality clients.
Escape the corporate fluff—we focus on meaningful campaigns, proactive insights, and real results that keep clients partnering with us for years.
Opportunity to rapidly expand your operational responsibilities from quality control into full campaign ownership.

description:
About the job

About Cosign

Cosign helps property owners approve more qualified renters by reducing application denials without increasing risk. We work with leading multifamily operators and are backed by some of the most respected investors and operators in real estate and technology.




About the Role

This is not a brand marketing, content marketing, social media, events, paid marketing, or generalist growth role. We are looking for an exceptional email operator who lives and breathes outbound email, deliverability, segmentation, campaign operations, and lifecycle email programs. Email is one of Cosign's most important go-to-market channels. C1/C2 English proficiency required. This role involves daily written and verbal communication with English-speaking management, vendors, and internal teams.




Our goal is to execute targeted sub-campaigns based on key market triggers such as occupancy changes, new supply, ownership changes, management changes, and other relevant signals. We are looking for someone who can build the systems, processes, segmentation, deliverability, and operational rigor required to make that possible.




This role requires someone who can bring order to complexity. As our email volume grows, success will depend as much on organization, campaign governance, audience management, data hygiene, and operational discipline as it does on messaging and campaign strategy. You will serve as the single owner of outbound email performance at Cosign, managing campaign execution, audience segmentation, deliverability, email infrastructure, data hygiene, personalization, and performance optimization.




What You'll Own




Campaign Execution & Management

Build, launch, monitor, and optimize 10+ concurrent outbound email campaigns
Own the campaign operating rhythm required to consistently reach Cosign’s ICP on a monthly cadence
Build and manage targeted campaigns based on occupancy changes, new supply, ownership changes, management changes, and other market signals
Manage campaign calendars, schedules, and audience assignments
Coordinate with Sales and leadership on messaging, targeting, campaign goals, and audience priorities
Own campaign QA, launch processes, and execution standards
Ensure campaigns are executed accurately, on time, and at scale

Performance Optimization

Analyze campaign performance and provide actionable recommendations to improve open rates, reply rates, engagement, targeting, and messaging effectiveness
Run ongoing A/B testing across subject lines, messaging, personalization, audience segmentation, and send timing
Create weekly reporting on key email metrics, campaign performance, audience learnings, deliverability trends, and recommended next steps

Audience Management & Data Hygiene

Own audience segmentation strategy, campaign targeting, and ICP coverage
Manage suppression logic, exclusion rules, contact hygiene, and audience governance
Prevent duplicate outreach, audience overlap, and over-contacting
Support email journeys across acquisition, nurture, engagement, and retention, ensuring communications are coordinated and measurable

Deliverability & Email Health

Monitor sender reputation, inbox placement, domain health, bounce rates, spam complaints, and unsubscribe rates
Maintain deliverability best practices as Cosign scales toward 150k+ outbound emails per month
Partner with technical teams on SPF, DKIM, DMARC, domain setup, and email infrastructure
Identify and resolve deliverability issues before they impact campaign performance or sender reputation
Support domain warming, suppression management, and sending infrastructure decisions

AI-Assisted Personalization

Use AI, CRM data, and campaign insights to improve email relevance and performance
Build scalable personalization workflows across audiences and campaigns
Create repeatable processes that improve personalization without sacrificing operational efficiency
Use campaign learnings to improve messaging, targeting, and audience segmentation

Email Operations

Own email operations within HubSpot, Apollo, and related CRM, enrichment, and marketing automation systems
Build scalable workflows, automations, campaign checklists, and QA processes
Maintain documentation, operating procedures, and campaign governance standards
Ensure data accuracy across CRM and outbound systems
Create visibility into campaign performance, campaign ownership, and active outreach efforts




Who You Are

Exceptionally organized, detail-oriented, and operationally rigorous
Obsessed with email performance, deliverability, audience quality, and campaign execution
Passionate about clean data, clear processes, documentation, and scalable systems
Naturally create workflows, checklists, SOPs, QA processes, and campaign governance
Analytical, performance-driven, and highly accountable
Comfortable managing multiple campaigns, audiences, stakeholders, and timelines at once
Able to create structure where none exists and push back when campaigns create audience conflict, data quality issues, or deliverability risk
Continuously look for ways to improve results through testing, optimization, and experimentation
Take pride in making email a reliable, measurable, and scalable growth channel

Requirements

3+ years managing outbound email programs in a high-growth B2B SaaS, marketplace, PropTech, or outbound-heavy environment
Experience managing multiple email campaigns at scale from strategy through execution
Strong understanding of segmentation, deliverability, audience governance, and email operations
Experience with HubSpot, Apollo, or similar CRM and outbound engagement platforms
Experience improving open rates, reply rates, and overall campaign performance
Experience building email workflows, sequences, and campaign automation
Experience using AI and data to personalize communications
Excellent written communication skills

Preferred

Lifecycle marketing experience
Experience managing email programs inside HubSpot or a similar CRM/marketing automation platform
Experience improving inbox placement and deliverability
Experience implementing audience governance and data hygiene frameworks
Experience scaling outbound email volume while maintaining strong performance
Experience partnering with Sales, Growth, RevOps, and Customer Success teams
Experience in fintech, insur-tech, financial services, or another relationship-driven B2B market

Success in This Role

Cosign consistently reaches priority ICP segments on a defined monthly cadence
Every outbound campaign is organized, documented, QA’d, launched on time, and measured
Open rates, reply rates, engagement, and campaign quality improve quarter over quarter
Leadership has clear visibility into what campaigns are live, who they target, who owns them, and how they are performing
Duplicate outreach, audience overlap, conflicting campaigns, and over-contacting are eliminated
Deliverability remains strong as outbound volume scales toward 150k+ emails per month
Email becomes a reliable, measurable, and scalable growth channel for the business
Cosign builds a best-in-class outbound email engine capable of reaching the right audience with the right message at the right time

title: Talent Partner
skills: Oversee the entire end-to-end recruitment process for different business departments, with a particular emphasis on high-volume hiring in operational and retail roles
Partner closely with hiring managers and their teams to ensure smooth, efficient hiring processes
Establish and maintain meaningful relationships with hiring managers and key stakeholders
Own and drive the offer process from beginning to end, while providing a world-class experience to your candidates that aligns with our values
Promote our employer brand, champion the Flix culture and ensure we hire the right people for the right team who also align with our mission and values
Maintain high data quality and standards across all our tools and systems (e.g., Greenhouse)
3–5 years of work experience in end-to-end recruiting
Experience in high-volume hiring (e.g., operational, retail, customer service or similar roles)
Strong offer and stakeholder management skills, with ability to make data-driven decisions
Sound appreciation for the fundamentals of talent acquisition, but creative and inquisitive when considering modern tactics
Proactive working style with a keen eye for detail and quality
Fluent in Spanish; good command of English
Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.
description:
About the job

At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable. 

As we continue our expansion in Mexico, we're looking for a motivated and driven Recruiter (m/f/d), who will join our LATAM Talent Acquisition team and focus on recruiting for our business departments, with a strong emphasis on high-volume hiring for operations, shops, and retail staff.

This role will be based in Mexico City under a hybrid working model.

About The Role


Oversee the entire end-to-end recruitment process for different business departments, with a particular emphasis on high-volume hiring in operational and retail roles
Partner closely with hiring managers and their teams to ensure smooth, efficient hiring processes
Establish and maintain meaningful relationships with hiring managers and key stakeholders
Own and drive the offer process from beginning to end, while providing a world-class experience to your candidates that aligns with our values
Promote our employer brand, champion the Flix culture and ensure we hire the right people for the right team who also align with our mission and values
Maintain high data quality and standards across all our tools and systems (e.g., Greenhouse)


About You


3–5 years of work experience in end-to-end recruiting
Experience in high-volume hiring (e.g., operational, retail, customer service or similar roles)
Strong offer and stakeholder management skills, with ability to make data-driven decisions
Sound appreciation for the fundamentals of talent acquisition, but creative and inquisitive when considering modern tactics
Proactive working style with a keen eye for detail and quality
Fluent in Spanish; good command of English 


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

Fluent in Spanish; good command of English 


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

title: Digital Ad Insertion Coordinator
description:
About the job

The Digital Insertion Coordinator is responsible for the accurate execution, monitoring, and control of digital advertising campaigns across video and display platforms, ensuring insertion orders are implemented correctly and aligned with commercial and technical requirements. This role is critical to campaign delivery, KPI fulfillment, and coordination between Sales, Ad Operations, and Technology teams.




Key Responsibilities:

1. Insertion Order (IO) Management

- Create, validate, and manage digital insertion orders.

- Review commercial and technical specifications.

- Ensure campaigns are implemented as sold.

- Track changes and campaign adjustments.




2. Campaign Execution & Monitoring

- Monitor daily delivery of digital campaigns.

- Identify pacing or delivery issues.

- Coordinate basic optimizations.

- Escalate operational or technical issues.




3. Coordination with Ad Operations & Technology

- Support proper campaign setup in ad servers.

- Validate creatives, tags, and trackers.

- Ensure correct platform integrations.

- Assist in resolving delivery errors.




4. Reporting & Campaign Close

- Track key metrics (impressions, fill rate, pacing).

- Produce internal operational reports.

- Support campaign reconciliation and close.

- Maintain organized campaign documentation.




5. Process Improvement

- Identify opportunities to improve workflows.

- Ensure adherence to internal processes and SLAs.

- Support standardization of operational flows.




Requirements:

Hard Skills

- 2–4 years of experience in digital insertion, Ad Ops, or trafficking roles.

- Knowledge of digital advertising (display, video, CTV, OTT).

- Experience with ad servers (Google Ad Manager preferred).

- Understanding of insertion orders and pacing.

- Intermediate Excel / Google Sheets skills.

- Knowledge of digital performance metrics.




Soft Skills

- Strong attention to detail.

- Organization and follow-up skills.

- Clear communication.

- Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.

- Proactive problem-solving mindset.




Key KPIs

- Campaign delivery accuracy.

- Reduction of insertion errors.

- Time to campaign launch.

- Operational incidents.

- Reporting accuracy.
